item.title: Standing to one side, the devata Sudatta spoke this verse before the Blessed One: Like a sword touching the skin, Like fire burning the head,…
item.description:
Standing to one side, the devata Sudatta spoke this verse before the Blessed One:
Like a sword touching the skin,
Like fire burning the head,
A bhikkhu should be mindful,
Gone forth, abandoning sensual desires.
( Blessed One ):
Like a sword touching the skin,
Like fire burning the head,
A bhikkhu should be mindful,
Gone forth, abandoning identity view.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 2.16
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, there are these three kinds of good conduct. What three? Good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, and good mental conduct. Bhikkhus, these are the…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, there are these three kinds of good conduct. What three? Good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, and good mental conduct. Bhikkhus, these are the three kinds of good conduct.

Abandoning evil bodily action,
Abandoning evil verbal action,
Abandoning evil mental action,
And whatever else is blameworthy,

Not doing evil,
Doing much that is wholesome,
At the breaking up of the body,
One of superior wisdom
Is reborn in heaven.

From: Itivuttaka 65
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, what is wrong view? There is no giving, no offering, no sacrifice; no fruit or result of good and bad actions; no this…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, what is wrong view? There is no giving, no offering, no sacrifice; no fruit or result of good and bad actions; no this world, no next world; no mother, no father; no spontaneously reborn beings; no samaṇas or brahmins in the world who, having practiced rightly, have realized and declared this world and the next by their own direct knowledge. Thus, bhikkhus, is wrong view.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 117 – Mahācattārīsakasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth. What ten? One who has gone forth should constantly…
item.description:
- Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth. What ten? One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'I have now entered the state of being casteless.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'My livelihood depends on others.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'My deportment should be different (from laypeople).' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do I reproach myself regarding virtue?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do my wise fellow celibates, after examination, reproach me regarding virtue?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'All things that are dear and agreeable to me are subject to change and alteration.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'I am the owner of my kamma, heir of my kamma, born of my kamma, related to my kamma, supported by my kamma; whatever kamma I will do, good or evil, of that I will be the heir.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'How do I spend my days and nights? What has become of me?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do I delight in empty huts or not?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Have I attained a superior human state, a truly noble distinction of knowledge and vision, so that when questioned by my fellow celibates on my last days, I will not be ashamed?' Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 10.48
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Heraññakāni: Day and night pass, life diminishes, Human life-span decays like water in a small stream. The fool doing evil deeds does not understand…
item.description:
Elder Heraññakāni:

Day and night pass, life diminishes,
Human life-span decays like water in a small stream.

The fool doing evil deeds does not understand his own action.
Afterwards he suffers when the evil ripens.

From: Theragāthā 2.13
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Khaṅkhārevata: See this wisdom of the Tathāgatas, Like a fire burning at midnight, Giving light, giving sight, They subdue the doubts of those who…
item.description:
Elder Khaṅkhārevata:

See this wisdom of the Tathāgatas,
Like a fire burning at midnight,
Giving light, giving sight,
They subdue the doubts of those who come.

From: Theragāthā 1.3
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - A fellow bhikkhu, Venerable Sāriputta, has given up the training and returned to the lower life." - "Friend, that happens to one who does…
item.description:
- A fellow bhikkhu, Venerable Sāriputta, has given up the training and returned to the lower life." - "Friend, that happens to one who does not guard the sense faculties, does not know moderation in eating, and is not devoted to wakefulness. 'Friend, a bhikkhu who does not guard the sense faculties, does not know moderation in eating, and is not devoted to wakefulness — for life, that one could live the holy life completely pure — that is impossible.' 'Friend, a bhikkhu who guards the sense faculties, knows moderation in eating, and is devoted to wakefulness — for life, that one could live the holy life completely pure — that is possible. Therefore, friend, you should train thus: "We will guard the sense faculties, know moderation in eating, and be devoted to wakefulness." Thus, friend, you should train.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 35.120
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Just as, Venerable Sir, a person carries a bowl full of oil, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. So too, Venerable Sir, I carry…
item.description:
Just as, Venerable Sir, a person carries a bowl full of oil, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. So too, Venerable Sir, I carry this body, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. Venerable Sir, for one who does not have mindfulness of the body established, if he offends a fellow celibate, he might go off wandering without apologizing.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 9.11
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: He possesses the virtue loved by the noble ones, unbroken, unblemished, untarnished, continuously practiced, without flaw, leading to freedom, praised by the wise, not defiled,…
item.description:
He possesses the virtue loved by the noble ones, unbroken, unblemished, untarnished, continuously practiced, without flaw, leading to freedom, praised by the wise, not defiled, conducive to concentration.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 33 – Saṅgītisutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, Venerable Sir, I see bhikkhus joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild…
item.description:
Furthermore, Venerable Sir, I see bhikkhus joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild deer. Venerable Sir, I think: 'Surely these venerable ones have realized some supreme, excellent distinction in the Blessed One's teaching, a progressive attainment, that they are so joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild deer.' Venerable Sir, this is my conviction regarding the Blessed One: 'The Blessed One is perfectly enlightened; the Dhamma is well taught by the Blessed One; the Saṅgha of the Blessed One's disciples is well practiced and well trained.'

From: Majjhima Nikāya 89 – Dhammacetiyasutta
📖 Read this sutta
